volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Prometheus Bound (Section 1 - Section 1.135)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: Χθονὸς μὲν ἐς τηλουρὸν ἥκομεν πέδον ,
sections.translation: To earth ’ s remotest limit we come , to the Scythian land , an untrodden solitude . And now , Hephaestus , yours is the charge to observe the mandates laid upon you by the Father — to clamp this miscreant

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: ὑψηλοκρήμνοις τὸν λεωργὸν ὀχμάσαι
sections.translation: upon the high craggy rocks in shackles of binding adamant that cannot be broken . For your own flower , flashing fire , source of all arts , he has purloined and bestowed upon mortal creatures . Such is his offence ; for this he is bound to make requital to the gods ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ὡς ἂν διδαχθῇ τὴν Διὸς τυραννίδα
sections.translation: so that he may learn to bear with the sovereignty of Zeus and cease his man-loving ways .

sections.label: Section 12
sections.original: Κράτος Βία τε , σφῷν μὲν ἐντολὴ Διὸς
sections.translation: Power and Force , for you indeed the behest of Zeus is now fulfilled , and nothing remains to stop you . But for me — I do not have the nerve myself

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: δῆσαι βίᾳ φάραγγι πρὸς [*] δυσχειμέρῳ .
sections.translation:
to bind with force a kindred god upon this rocky cleft assailed by cruel winter . Yet , come what may , I am constrained to summon courage to this deed ; for it is perilous to disregard the commandments of the Father .
Lofty-minded son of Themis who counsels straight , against my will , no less than yours , I must rivet you with brazen bonds

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: προσπασσαλεύσω τῷδ ἀπανθρώπῳ πάγῳ [*]
sections.translation: no hand can loose to this desolate crag , where neither voice nor form of mortal man shall you perceive ; but , scorched by the sun ’ s bright beams , you shall lose the fair bloom of your flesh . And glad you shall be when spangled-robed night shall veil his brightness and

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: πάχνην θ ἑῴαν ἥλιος σκεδᾷ πάλιν :
sections.translation:
when the sun shall scatter again the frost of morning . Evermore the burden of your present ill shall wear you out ; for your deliverer is not yet born .
Such is the prize you have gained for your championship of man . For , god though you are , you did not fear the wrath of the gods , but

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: βροτοῖσι τιμὰς ὤπασας πέρα δίκης .
sections.translation: you bestowed honors upon mortal creatures beyond their due . Therefore on this joyless rock you must stand sentinel , erect , sleepless , your knee unbent . And many a groan and unavailing lament you shall utter ; for the heart of Zeus is hard ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: ἅπας δὲ τραχὺς ὅστις ἂν νέον κρατῇ .
sections.translation: and everyone is harsh whose power is new .

sections.label: Section 36
sections.original: εἶεν , τί μέλλεις καὶ κατοικτίζει μάτην ;
sections.translation: Well , why delay and excite pity in vain ? Why do you not detest a god most hateful to the gods , since he has betrayed your prerogative to mortals ?

sections.label: Section 39
sections.original: τὸ συγγενές τοι δεινὸν ἥ θ ὁμιλία .
sections.translation: A strangely potent tie is kinship , and companionship as well .

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: σύμφημ : ἀνηκουστεῖν δὲ τῶν πατρὸς λόγων [*]
sections.translation: I agree ; yet to refuse to obey the commands of the Father ; is this possible ? Do you not fear that more ?

sections.label: Section 42
sections.original: αἰεί γε [*] δὴ νηλὴς σὺ καὶ θράσους πλέως .
sections.translation: Yes , you are ever pitiless and steeped in insolence .

sections.label: Section 43
sections.original: ἄκος γὰρ οὐδὲν τόνδε θρηνεῖσθαι : σὺ δὲ
sections.translation: Yes , for it does not good to bemoan this fellow . Stop wasting your labor at an unprofitable task .

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ὦ πολλὰ μισηθεῖσα χειρωναξία .
sections.translation: Oh handicraft that I hate so much !

sections.label: Section 46
sections.original: τί νιν στυγεῖς ; πόνων γὰρ ὡς ἁπλῷ λόγῳ
sections.translation: Why hate it ? Since in truth your craft is in no way to blame for these present troubles .

sections.label: Section 48
sections.original: ἔμπας τις αὐτὴν ἄλλος ὤφελεν λαχεῖν .
sections.translation: Nevertheless , i wish it had fallen to another ’ s lot !

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: ἅπαντ ἐπαχθῆ [*] πλὴν θεοῖσι κοιρανεῖν .
sections.translation: Every job is troublesome except to be the commander of gods ;

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: ἐλεύθερος γὰρ οὔτις ἐστὶ πλὴν Διός .
sections.translation: no one is free except Zeus .

sections.label: Section 51
sections.original: ἔγνωκα τοῖσδε κοὐδὲν ἀντειπεῖν ἔχω .
sections.translation: I know it by this task ; I cannot deny it .

sections.label: Section 52
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐπείξει τῷδε δεσμὰ περιβαλεῖν ,
sections.translation: Hurry then to cast the fetters about him , so that the Father does not see you loitering .

sections.label: Section 54
sections.original: καὶ δὴ πρόχειρα ψάλια [*] δέρκεσθαι πάρα .
sections.translation: Well , there then ! The bands are ready , as you may see .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: βαλών [*] νιν ἀμφὶ χερσὶν ἐγκρατεῖ σθένει
sections.translation: Cast them about his wrists and with might strike with your hammer ; rivet him to the rocks .

sections.label: Section 57
sections.original: περαίνεται δὴ κοὐ ματᾷ τοὔργον τόδε .
sections.translation: There ! The work is getting done and not improperly .

sections.label: Section 58
sections.original: ἄρασσε μᾶλλον , σφίγγε , μηδαμῇ χάλα .
sections.translation: Strike harder , clamp him tight , leave nothing loose ; for he is wondrously clever at finding a way even out of desperate straits .

sections.label: Section 60
sections.original: ἄραρεν ἥδε γ ὠλένη δυσεκλύτως .
sections.translation: This arm , at least , is fixed permanently .

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: καὶ τήνδε νῦν πόρπασον ἀσφαλῶς , ἵνα
sections.translation: Now rivet this one too and securely , so that he may learn , for all his cleverness , that he is a fool compared to Zeus .

sections.label: Section 63
sections.original: πλὴν τοῦδ ἂν οὐδεὶς ἐνδίκως μέμψαιτό μοι .
sections.translation: None but he could justly blame my work .

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: ἀδαμαντίνου νῦν σφηνὸς αὐθάδη γνάθον
sections.translation: Now drive the adamantine wedge ’ s stubborn edge straight

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: στέρνων διαμπὰξ πασσάλευ ἐρρωμένως .
sections.translation: through his chest with your full force .

sections.label: Section 66
sections.original: αἰαῖ , Προμηθεῦ , σῶν ὑπερστένω [*] πόνων .
sections.translation: Alas , Prometheus , I groan for your sufferings .

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: σὺ δ αὖ κατοκνεῖς τῶν Διός τ ἐχθρῶν ὕπερ
sections.translation: What ! Shrinking again and groaning over the enemies of Zeus ? Take care , so that the day does not come when you shall grieve for yourself .

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: ὁρᾷς θέαμα δυσθέατον ὄμμασιν .
sections.translation: You see a spectacle grievous for eyes to behold .

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ὁρῶ κυροῦντα τόνδε τῶν ἐπαξίων . [*]
sections.translation: I see this man getting his deserts . Come , cast the girths about his sides .

sections.label: Section 72
sections.original: δρᾶν ταῦτ ἀνάγκη , μηδὲν ἐγκέλευ ἄγαν .
sections.translation: I must do this ; spare me your needless ordering .

sections.label: Section 73
sections.original: ἦ μὴν κελεύσω κἀπιθωύξω γε πρός .
sections.translation: Indeed , I ’ ll order you , yes and more — I ’ ll hound you on . Get down below , and ring his legs by force .

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: καὶ δὴ πέπρακται τοὔργον οὐ μακρῷ πόνῳ .
sections.translation: There now ! The work ’ s done and without much labor .

sections.label: Section 76
sections.original: ἐρρωμένως νῦν θεῖνε διατόρους πέδας :
sections.translation: Now hammer the piercing fetters with your full force ; for the appraiser of our work is severe .

sections.label: Section 78
sections.original: ὅμοια μορφῇ γλῶσσά σου γηρύεται .
sections.translation: The utterance of your tongue matches your looks .

sections.label: Section 79
sections.original: σὺ μαλθακίζου , τὴν δ ἐμὴν αὐθαδίαν
sections.translation: Be softhearted then ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: ὀργῆς τε τραχυτῆτα μὴ πίπλησσέ μοι .
sections.translation: but do not attack my stubborn will and my harsh mood .

sections.label: Section 81
sections.original: στείχωμεν : ὡς κώλοισιν ἀμφίβληστρ ἔχει .
sections.translation: Let us be gone , since he has got the fetters on his limbs . Exit

sections.label: Section 82
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα νῦν ὕβριζε καὶ θεῶν γέρα
sections.translation: There now , indulge your insolence , keep on wresting from the gods their honors to give them to creatures of a day . Are mortals able to lighten your load of sorrow ?

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: ψευδωνύμως σε δαίμονες Προμηθέα
sections.translation: Falsely the gods call you Prometheus , [*] for you yourself need forethought to free yourself from this handiwork . Exeunt Power and Force

sections.label: Section 88
sections.original: ὦ δῖος [*] αἰθὴρ καὶ ταχύπτεροι πνοαί ,
sections.translation: O you bright sky of heaven , you swift-winged breezes , you river-waters , and

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original: ἀνήριθμον γέλασμα , παμμῆτόρ [*] τε γῆ ,
sections.translation: infinite laughter of the waves of ocean , O universal mother Earth , and you , all-seeing orb of the sun , to you I call ! See what I , a god , endure from the gods .

sections.label: Section 93
sections.original: δέρχθηθ οἵαις αἰκίαισιν
sections.translation: Look , with what shameful torture I am racked and must wrestle

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original: χρόνον ἀθλεύσω .
sections.translation: throughout the countless years of time apportioned me . Such is the ignominious bondage the new commander of the blessed has devised against me . Woe ! Woe ! For present misery and misery to come I groan , not knowing where

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original: χρὴ τέρματα τῶνδ ἐπιτεῖλαι .
sections.translation: it is fated that deliverance from these sorrows shall arise .

sections.label: Section 101
sections.original: καίτοι τί φημι ; πάντα προυξεπίσταμαι
sections.translation: And yet , what am I saying ? All that is to be I know full well and in advance , nor shall any affliction come upon me unforeseen . I must bear my allotted doom as lightly as I can , knowing that

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: τὸ τῆς ἀνάγκης ἔστ ἀδήριτον σθένος .
sections.translation: the might of Necessity permits no resistance . Yet I am not able to speak nor be silent about my fate . For it is because I bestowed good gifts on mortals that this miserable yoke of constraint has been bound upon me . I hunted out and stored in fennel stalk the stolen

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original: πηγὴν κλοπαίαν , ἣ διδάσκαλος τέχνης
sections.translation: source of fire that has proved a teacher to mortals in every art and a means to mighty ends . Such is the offence for which I pay the penalty , riveted in fetters beneath the open sky .

sections.label: Section 114
sections.original: ἆ ἆ [ ἔα ἔα ]. [*]
sections.translation: Ha ! Behold !

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: τίς ἀχώ , τίς ὀδμὰ προσέπτα μ ἀφεγγής ,
sections.translation: What murmur , what scent wings to me , its source invisible , heavenly or human , or both ? Has someone come to this crag at the edge of the world to stare at my sufferings — or with what motive ? Behold me , an ill-fated god , chained ,

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: τὸν Διὸς ἐχθρόν , τὸν πᾶσι θεοῖς
sections.translation: the foe of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: δι ἀπεχθείας ἐλθόνθ ὁπόσοι
sections.translation: hated of all who enter the court of Zeus , because of my very great love for mankind . Ha ! What ’ s this ? What may be this rustling stir of birds I hear

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: πέλας οἰωνῶν ; αἰθὴρ δ ἐλαφραῖς
sections.translation: again nearby ? The air whirs with the light rush of wings . Whatever approaches causes me alarm . The Daughters of Oceanus enter on a winged car

sections.label: Section 1.128
sections.original: μηδὲν φοβηθῇς : φιλία γὰρ ἅδε [*] τάξις
sections.translation: Do not fear ! For our group has come in swift rivalry of wings to this crag

sections.label: Section 1.130
sections.original: προσέβα τόνδε πάγον , πατρῴας
sections.translation: as friend to you , having won our father ’ s consent as best we might . The swift-coursing breezes bore me on ; for the reverberation of the clang of iron pierced the depths of our caves and drove my grave modesty away in fright ;

sections.label: Section 1.135
sections.original: σύθην δ ἀπέδιλος ὄχῳ πτερωτῷ .
sections.translation: unsandalled I have hastened in a winged car .
